author: gb9t
content: Iteration 36: vfio/vfio-pci.md committed (b185577, 231 lines). vfio-pci — PCIe passthrough foundation. Every KVM/QEMU VM with SR-IOV VF, GPU passthrough, NVMe passthrough flows through this. ~32K LOC core + 6 per-vendor migration variants. Most security-critical boundary in kernel — grants tenant VMs direct hardware MMIO + DMA; bugs = cross-tenant escape. Defenses: IOMMU isolation MANDATORY (closes 'tenant in shared group DMA-attacks neighbor'), config-space filter strict per-capability hand-curated (closes CVE-2021-32606-class), BUS_MASTER gating, MSI-X table virtualization + MSI-X BAR mmap refused in MSI-X-enabled state (closes 'guest writes arbitrary IRQ vectors'), eventfd refcount strict (closes CVE-2024-26851-class), hot reset peer-fd verification, migration state machine atomic via typestate, per-vendor variant binding restricted, dma-buf opt-in, migration buffer integrity signed/verified. 36 docs / ~2.05M LOC. Next iteration: drivers/iommu/intel/ — Intel VT-d IOMMU (foundational for PCIe DMA isolation across every Intel system, prerequisite for vfio-pci defenses).
